[File: 'src/ratifiers/interfaces/IEcrecoverRatifier.sol'] [Function: isRatified] Can a taker call Midnight.take() with a valid offer where the offer's expiry is exactly at block.timestamp (boundary condition), causing Midnight.take to revert OfferExpired (require block.timestamp <= offer.expiry fails if expiry == block.timestamp - 1), blocking the take even though the offer was valid one second earlier? Preconditions: maker signs offer with expiry = T; taker submits take at block.timestamp = T+1. Call sequence: taker calls Midnight.take(offer_with_expiry_T, ratifierData, ...) at block.timestamp = T+1; Midnight checks block.timestamp <= offer.expiry which fails; reverts OfferExpired before calling isRatified. Invariant tested: offer expiry boundary must be correctly handled; offers must be fillable up to and including expiry timestamp. Scoped impact: fund freeze — taker cannot fill valid offer if block.timestamp > offer.expiry. Proof idea: fuzz block.timestamp around offer.expiry and assert take succeeds at expiry and fails at expiry+1.

[File: 'src/ratifiers/interfaces/IEcrecoverRatifier.sol'] [Function: isRatified] Can a taker call Midnight.take() with a valid offer where the offer's start is in the future (block.timestamp < offer.start), causing Midnight.take to revert OfferNotStarted before calling isRatified, blocking the take even though the offer is correctly signed and ratified? Preconditions: maker signs offer with start = T+100; taker submits take at block.timestamp = T. Call sequence: taker calls Midnight.take(offer_with_start_T_plus_100, ratifierData, ...) at block.timestamp = T; Midnight checks block.timestamp >= offer.start which fails; reverts OfferNotStarted before calling isRatified. Invariant tested: offer start boundary must be correctly handled; offers must not be fillable before start timestamp. Scoped impact: fund freeze — taker cannot fill valid offer before start timestamp; this is correct behavior but must be tested at boundary. Proof idea: fuzz block.timestamp around offer.start and assert take fails before start and succeeds at start.

[File: 'src/ratifiers/interfaces/IEcrecoverRatifier.sol'] [Function: isRatified] Can a taker call Midnight.take() with a valid offer where the market's lossFactor has reached type(uint128).max, causing Midnight.take to revert MarketLossFactorMaxedOut before calling isRatified, permanently blocking all takes in that market even though offers are correctly signed and ratified? Scoped impact: fund freeze — all takes in maxed-out market are permanently blocked. Proof idea: unit test with lossFactor = type(uint128).max and assert MarketLossFactorMaxedOut revert.

[File: 'src/ratifiers/interfaces/IEcrecoverRatifier.sol'] [Function: isRatified] Can a taker call Midnight.take() with a valid offer where the offer's maker is the same as the taker (self-take), causing Midnight.take to revert SelfTake before calling isRatified, blocking the take even though the offer is correctly signed and ratified? Call sequence: taker calls Midnight.take(offer_with_maker_equals_taker, ratifierData, ...) with taker == offer.maker; Midnight checks offer.maker != taker which fails; reverts SelfTake before calling isRatified. Invariant tested: self-take must be blocked; isRatified must not be called for self-takes. Scoped impact: fund freeze — maker cannot fill their own offer even if correctly signed. Proof idea: unit test with maker == taker and assert SelfTake revert.

[File: 'src/ratifiers/interfaces/IEcrecoverRatifier.sol'] [Function: isRatified] Can a taker call Midnight.take() with a valid offer where the offer's market has not been created yet (marketState.tickSpacing == 0 before touchMarket), causing touchMarket to create the market with default fee parameters, and if the default settlement fee is higher than the offer's tick price, causing the take to revert, blocking the take even though the offer is correctly signed? Preconditions: maker signs offer for a market that doesn't exist yet; default settlement fee is set high; offer.tick price < settlement fee. Call sequence: taker calls Midnight.take(offer, ratifierData, ...); touchMarket creates market with high default fee; settlement fee computation causes sellerPrice to underflow or buyerPrice to exceed tick price; take reverts. Invariant tested: market creation at take time must not invalidate previously signed offers; settlement fee must not retroactively block valid offers. Scoped impact: fund freeze — valid offers blocked if market is created with high default fee at take time. Proof idea: unit test with high default fee and assert take reverts for low-tick offers.

[File: 'src/ratifiers/interfaces/IEcrecoverRatifier.sol'] [Function: isRatified] Can a taker call Midnight.take() with a valid offer where the offer's market has both maxAssets and maxUnits set to non-zero values, causing Midnight.take to revert MultipleNonZero before calling isRatified, blocking the take even though the offer is correctly signed and ratified? [File: 'src/ratifiers/interfaces/IEcrecoverRatifier.sol'] [Function: isRatified] Can a taker call Midnight.take() with a valid offer where the seller (borrower) is already liquidatable (SellerIsLiquidatable check in Midnight), causing the take to revert before calling isRatified, blocking the take even though the offer is correctly signed and ratified, and preventing the seller from reducing their debt via a sell offer? Scoped impact: fund freeze — seller cannot reduce debt via sell offer if already liquidatable. Proof idea: unit test with liquidatable seller and sell offer; assert SellerIsLiquidatable revert.

[File: 'src/ratifiers/interfaces/IEcrecoverRatifier.sol'] [Function: isRatified] Can a taker call Midnight.take() with a valid offer where the buyer's enter gate (enterGate.canIncreaseCredit) reverts or returns false, causing the take to revert before or after calling isRatified, blocking the take even though the offer is correctly signed and ratified? Scoped impact: fund freeze — valid take blocked by enter gate even though offer is correctly ratified. Proof idea: unit test with restrictive enter gate and assert BuyerGatedFromIncreasingCredit revert after isRatified succeeds.

[File: 'src/ratifiers/interfaces/IEcrecoverRatifier.sol'] [Function: isRatified] Can a taker call Midnight.take() with a valid offer where the maker callback (offer.callback) reverts or returns wrong value, causing the take to revert after isRatified succeeds, blocking the take even though the offer is correctly signed and ratified, and leaving the consumed amount incremented without the take completing? [File: 'src/ratifiers/interfaces/IEcrecoverRatifier.sol'] [Function: isRatified] Can a taker call Midnight.take() with a valid offer where the loan token's transferFrom reverts (e.g., insufficient allowance, fee-on-transfer token returning false), causing the take to revert after isRatified succeeds, blocking the take even though the offer is correctly signed and ratified? [File: 'src/ratifiers/interfaces/IEcrecoverRatifier.sol'] [Function: isRatified] Can a taker call Midnight.take() with a valid offer where the offer's market has a debt increase after maturity (offer.buy == false, seller's debt would increase, block.timestamp > market.maturity), causing Midnight.take to revert CannotIncreaseDebtPostMaturity before calling isRatified, blocking the take even though the offer is correctly signed and ratified? [File: 'src/ratifiers/interfaces/IEcrecoverRatifier.sol'] [Function: isRatified] Can a taker call Midnight.take() with a valid offer where the offer's market has an unhealthy borrower (seller) who is also the maker, causing Midnight.take to revert UnhealthyBorrower before calling isRatified, blocking the take even though the offer is correctly signed and ratified? [File: 'src/ratifiers/interfaces/IEcrecoverRatifier.sol'] [Function: isRatified] Can a taker call Midnight.take() with a valid offer where the offer's market has a TooManyActivatedCollaterals error (seller has too many activated collateral types), causing the take to revert after isRatified succeeds, blocking the take even though the offer is correctly signed and ratified?
